$schema_note: Diacritic + context disambiguation rules for high-frequency Arabic function-word homographs that norm() collapses. Each rule keys on the harakah of the CONTENT letter (read AFTER any و/ف proclitic), never the first letter. Consume with tools.normalize_ar: haraka_on(tok, letter), shadda_on(tok, letter), is_man_who(tok). FATHA=064E, KASRA=0650, DAMMA=064F, SHADDA=0651. A rule emits decision_if_A / decision_if_B; if the diacritic is absent (undiacritized input) emit the pending fallback. PENDING beats a wrong gloss.
generator: authored by hand; validate field names against nahw/examples/function-word-decisions.jsonl

distinguish_by.also: shadda_on(tok, 'ن') guards the verb مَنَّ 'to bestow'

decision_if_A.when: no kasra on mīm (fatḥa or bare) AND no shadda on nūn
decision_if_A.reason: fatḥa (or bare) on the mīm content letter → pronoun, not the preposition مِنْ

decision_if_B.when: kasra on mīm (incl. وَمِنَ where the و proclitic shifts the harakah right)
decision_if_B.reason: kasra on the mīm content letter → preposition; the leading و/ف does not move the decision letter

pending_fallback.note: undiacritized input: مَن/مِن indistinguishable without the content-letter harakah

rules.guard: وَمِنَ is NOT وَمَن; وَمَنَّ (shadda on nūn) is the verb 'to bestow', neither pronoun nor preposition

distinguish_by.also: sukūn on mīm marks لَمْ; fatḥa on mīm marks لِمَ

decision_if_A.when: fatḥa on lām (and the following verb is jussive)
decision_if_A.reason: negation of the past via jussive; e.g. لَمْ يَلِدْ

pending_fallback.note: without the lām harakah, لَمْ 'did not' and لِمَ 'why' are not separable

distinguish_by.signal: hamza seat (أ vs إ) — preserved by norm_strict(), dropped by norm()
distinguish_by.also: shadda_on(tok, 'ن') separates the emphatic أَنَّ/إِنَّ from light أَنْ/إِنْ

decision_if_A.reason: fatḥa-seat hamza → أَنْ/أَنَّ; light أَنْ takes subjunctive, أَنَّ (shadda) takes a noun-clause

decision_if_B.reason: kasra-seat hamza → إِنَّ/إِنْ; shadda marks the emphatic, light إِنْ is conditional or negating

pending_fallback.note: if norm() dropped the hamza seat, re-read with norm_strict before deciding أَنْ vs إِنَّ

distinguish_by.signal: kasra cluster on nūn → أَنِّي; fatḥa on nūn → أَنَّى
distinguish_by.also: final yāʾ ـِي (1sg pronoun) vs final alif-maqṣūra ـَى

decision_if_A.reason: kasra cluster on the nūn; e.g. أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ

decision_if_B.reason: fatḥa on nūn → adverbial أَنَّى, not the pronoun-bearing أَنِّي

pending_fallback.note: without the nūn harakah, أَنِّي 'that I' vs أَنَّى 'how' is undecidable

distinguish_by.also: kasra on lām marks the لِ + relative مَا reading

decision_if_A.reason: shadda on the mīm marks لَمَّا; e.g. فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ

decision_if_B.when: no shadda on mīm (kasra on lām)
decision_if_B.reason: لِ + relative مَا, no shadda; e.g. مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ

pending_fallback.note: the shadda on the mīm is the decisive mark; if absent in input, do not guess 'when'

distinguish_by.also: ḍamma on kāf → كُلّ 'all'; fatḥa on kāf → كَلَّا 'nay'

decision_if_A.reason: ḍamma on the kāf content letter → quantifier; e.g. وَيَعْقُوبَ كُلًّا هَدَيْنَا

decision_if_B.gloss: nay / by no means / not so
decision_if_B.reason: fatḥa on the kāf → the rebuke particle كَلَّا, not the quantifier كُلّ

pending_fallback.note: kāf harakah (ḍamma vs fatḥa) is the only separator; do not default to 'all'

distinguish_by.also: kasra on nūn + sukūn on ʿayn → نِعْمَ; fatḥa on nūn → نَعَمْ

decision_if_A.reason: kasra on nūn marks the praise verb; e.g. نِعْمَ الثَّوَابُ

decision_if_B.reason: fatḥa on nūn → affirmation, not the praise verb نِعْمَ

pending_fallback.note: nūn harakah decides praise-verb vs 'yes'; undiacritized input stays pending
